name: SuperPawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across the U.S., UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans secured by personal property and retail merchandise sales.
description_long: FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the largest international pawn store chain with over 3,300 retail locations spanning 29 U.S. states, Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). The company was founded to serve cash and credit-constrained consumers who need fast access to funds or merchandise options outside traditional banking. FirstCash employs approximately 22,000 people and is listed in both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, indicating significant scale and institutional recognition. The company also operates AFF, a subsidiary providing lease-to-own and retail finance solutions through 15,000+ merchant partner locations. FirstCash's core business model centers on non-recourse pawn loans secured by pledged personal property, meaning borrowers are not liable for deficiency if the collateral sells for less than the loan amount. The company buys and sells a wide variety of items including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. Beyond pawn loans, FirstCash offers layaway services (10% down payment required), gold and precious metal buying, retail merchandise sales, and a store locator service to help customers find nearby locations. The company distinguishes itself through its massive retail footprint—over 2,500 stores mentioned across North and Latin America—giving it unmatched convenience and inventory selection compared to smaller pawn operators. FirstCash's integration into major stock indices demonstrates institutional credibility and operational maturity. For consumers, FirstCash offers immediate cash access without credit checks or employment verification, making it attractive for those with poor credit or urgent liquidity needs. However, pawn loans come with inherent risks: borrowers lose collateral if they cannot repay, and while non-recourse protects them financially, the loss of personal property can be significant. The company's massive scale and corporate structure make it more standardized and less flexible than independent pawn shops, though this also means more consistent policies and protections.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, UK, and Latin America—unprecedented convenience and accessibility
Non-recourse pawn loans—borrowers are not liable if collateral sells for less than loan amount
No credit check required—serves credit-constrained consumers who cannot access traditional financing
Immediate cash on the spot—same-day funding for urgent financial needs
Wide variety of accepted collateral—jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, appliances, sporting goods
S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000 index company—institutional credibility and operational transparency
Layaway service available—10% down payment option for customers to purchase items without full upfront cost
cons: Loss of collateral if loan cannot be repaid—personal property forfeiture is permanent unless redeemed
Limited information on website about interest rates, loan terms, or fees—transparency issues for comparing costs
Pawn loans typically have short repayment terms (30-90 days standard in industry)—difficult for those needing longer-term solutions
Corporate standardization reduces flexibility—less negotiable terms compared to independent pawn shops
Selling collateral to fund loans perpetuates asset depletion for vulnerable consumers—may worsen long-term financial situation
best_for: Consumers with urgent cash needs and valuable collateral they can temporarily part with
Individuals with poor credit or no credit history who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans
People seeking quick inventory of used electronics, jewelry, tools, or instruments at discounted prices
Shoppers interested in layaway purchases with manageable 10% down payment commitments
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers facing immediate cash shortages who own valuable personal property and have exhausted credit-based options. The critical caveat is that pawn lending involves permanent loss of collateral upon non-repayment—this is debt financed through asset depletion, not income-based repayment, making it suitable only for short-term emergencies with items of genuine surplus value.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property
Gold and precious metal buying (immediate cash payment)
Retail sales of merchandise (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments)
Lease-to-own and retail finance solutions (through AFF subsidiary)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
